title: starter-notes v1
description: The starter's example entity: a note. Every write of the same noteId creates a NEW immutable droplet (a revision); the by-id-latest pointer index always resolves to the newest one. This is the canonical RainDB entity shape -- replace it with YOUR domain entity (a product, a post, a booking) when you build your app. There are no migrations: bump the schema version and publish.

authorName.description: Who wrote it. Indexed (by-author) so per-author listing is O(pageSize). NOTE: deliberately NOT named 'author' -- 'author' is the platform's reserved write-author template variable (the writing principal, e.g. bolt:<id>) and would shadow a payload field of the same name in index path templates.

tags.description: Free-form labels. Periscope SQL can filter on them.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601. Display only; chronology comes from the UUIDv7 noteId.
